pr.title: Matched HSD_SynthSFXGroupDataReaddress by rewriting the loop and pulling allocations out of the loop
pr.author: DavidWeaverAudio

summary: Matched HSD_SynthSFXGroupDataReaddress in src/sysdolphin/baselib/synth.c by changing local variable lifetimes and loop shape: count and j were declared outside the loop, a guarded do/while over count was rewritten as a for loop, and pointer advancement was expressed through a uintptr_t cast before adding the 0x10 header stride.
what_changed: In HSD_SynthSFXGroupDataReaddress, added outer-scope locals int count and int j rather than declaring count inside the while body.
Replaced an if (count > 0) guarded do/while (--count != 0) loop with for (j = 0; j < count; j++).
Preserved the body behavior: conditionally readdress *(u32*)(q + 0x14) when *(u16*)(q + 0x10) is nonzero, always readdress *(u32*)(q + 0x18) and *(u32*)(q + 0x1C), then advance q by 0x40.
Changed p advancement from p += (count << 6) to p = (u8*) ((count << 6) + (uintptr_t) p), followed by the existing p += 0x10.
The PR only touched src/sysdolphin/baselib/synth.c, with 11 added and 11 deleted lines.
smart_moves: Pulled loop variables out to outer scope, which can affect register allocation and stack/local lifetime enough to match without changing semantics.
Used an explicit induction variable j instead of destructive decrementing of count, allowing count to remain available for the later p stride calculation.
Rewrote pointer arithmetic through uintptr_t, a useful matching tactic when the compiler needs to emit integer-style address calculation rather than simple typed pointer addition.
Kept the readdressing logic byte-offset based, avoiding premature struct invention in a function that appears to depend on exact AXVPB/internal data layout offsets.
decomp_lessons: For matching, loop form matters: a guarded do/while and a for loop over the same range can compile differently even when semantically equivalent.
Do not assume declaring temporaries in the narrowest C scope will match; older compilers may require wider-scope locals to reproduce register allocation.
When a loop count is needed after iterating, preserving the original count with a separate index variable can match better than decrementing the count in-place.
Pointer updates involving byte offsets may need casts through uintptr_t to coax the desired arithmetic/codegen shape.
Small source-only changes in variable placement and arithmetic expression form can be sufficient to match a baselib function.
naming_conventions: Temporary byte pointers use short names p and q in this file/function.
Loop/index locals use short integer names i and j; count stores the per-group entry count.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Rewrite guarded do/while loops as for loops when instruction ordering or branch structure does not match.
Move local declarations out of inner scopes to influence allocation/lifetime.
Use a separate loop index to avoid mutating count when the post-loop code must still use the original count.
Express p advancement as (u8*) ((count << 6) + (uintptr_t) p) to alter generated pointer arithmetic.
Preserve exact byte offsets such as 0x10, 0x14, 0x18, 0x1C, and 0x40 when matching low-level data walking code.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context.
No issue comment body text was included in the slice, despite the metadata reporting one issue comment.
follow_up_queries: Find other synth.c functions where pointer arithmetic through uintptr_t improves matching.
Search for other HSD or baselib readdress functions using guarded do/while loops that may match as for loops.
Look for functions where moving count/index locals to outer scope changes register allocation for matching.
